access: I-Ride Trolley runs the full I-Drive corridor every 20 minutes. SunRail's Meadow Woods station is a short rideshare hop away for downtown trips.
night: I-Ride Trolley runs until midnight most nights; Lyft and Uber are plentiful on I-Drive after hours.
walk: Walkable within the I-Drive corridor — most attractions, restaurants, and hotels are within a 15-minute stroll of each other.
notes: Best base for theme-park visitors who want walkable nightlife. Rental car not needed if you stay on the corridor.

access: SunRail commuter rail connects downtown to Orlando Airport (OIA), Lake Mary to the north, and Kissimmee to the south. Central Station is the main hub.
night: SunRail runs limited evening service — check schedules. Rideshare is the go-to after 9 PM.
walk: Downtown core is walkable; the Milk District and Thornton Park are easy on foot from Central Station.
notes: Best for travelers who want a genuine local neighbourhood feel away from the tourist corridor.

access: Free Disney resort buses connect all on-property hotels to the parks and Disney Springs. No rental car needed if you stay on Disney property.
night: Disney transport runs until well after park close, though buses can be crowded. Disney's Minnie Van service offers a door-to-door rideshare option.
walk: Disney Springs is fully walkable; getting between resorts and parks requires the bus or Minnie Van.
notes: Staying on Disney property eliminates almost all transport friction for solo park visitors.

access: The Universal Cabana Bay and on-site hotels offer walking or shuttle access to Universal Orlando. Uber and Lyft are easy from the resort area.
night: CityWalk is open late; rideshare pickup is straightforward from the designated zones outside the gates.
walk: On-site Universal hotels are walking distance to the parks. Off-site hotels require shuttle or rideshare.
notes: Single-rider lines at Universal make solo visits especially efficient — waits can be dramatically shorter.
